opinion.h4: BOARD OF EDUCATION OF PARIS UNION SCHOOL DISTRICT NO. 95 ET AL. v. VAIL CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SEVENTH CIRCUIT No. 83-87. Argued February 28, 1984 Decided April 23, 1984
opinion.p: 706 F.2d 1435, affirmed by an equally divided Court.
Thomas R. Miller argued the cause and filed briefs for petitioners.
Marc J. Ansel argued the cause and filed a brief for respondent.*

f.p: Briefs of amici curiae urging affirmance were filed for the American Association of University Professors by Ralph S. Brown, Lawrence White, Ann H. Franke, and Victor J. Stone; and for the National Education Association et al. by Michael H. Gottesman, Robert M. Weinberg, and Charles S. Sims.
Gwendolyn H. Gregory filed a brief for the National School Boards Association as amicus curiae.
The judgment is affirmed by an equally divided Court.
JUSTICE MARSHALL took no part in the decision of this case.
